bull.b: A >3.5 billion daily user base with rising video engagement is a durable commercial moat: it sustains high ad impression volume, improves targeting data quality, and lowers marginal customer acquisition costs. That scale underpins long-term advertiser demand and pricing power across formats.

Very large operating cash flow and high margins provide durable financial flexibility to fund R&D, infrastructure and strategic initiatives without near-term capital constraints. Consistent cash generation supports investments, debt servicing and potential shareholder returns over the medium term.

Rapid adoption of GenAI tools and business-AI (millions of advertisers, >$20B run-rate) suggests structural revenue upside: AI features improve ad performance, raise ROI for marketers and deepen platform stickiness, increasing long-term monetization per user and creating product differentiation.

bear.b: A multi-year surge in capital spending and a $107B jump in commitments materially raises execution and ROIC risk: returns now depend on timely, high-return monetization of new AI infrastructure. If revenue per compute unit lags, cash flow and capital efficiency could be pressured for years.

Material increase in leverage reduces financial flexibility versus prior years and increases sensitivity to interest costs. Coupled with heavy capex and long-dated commitments, higher debt raises refinancing and covenant risk, constraining ability to respond to downturns or unexpected regulatory costs.

Elevated regulatory and litigation risk can force product changes, limit targeting/data use or impose fines, reducing ad effectiveness and revenues. Geographic access issues already affected daily actives; sustained regulatory constraints could structurally impair growth and raise compliance costs over multiple quarter
